methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

private.name: Shinkei
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Whisper Aero
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Daylight Computer is a Series A consumer tech company founded by Anjan Katta that has raised $10M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a limited comp universe of two private peers (Shinkei and Whisper Aero); no public comparables exist in this segment.

The stage-progression method was the applicable triangulation approach. Using total capital raised ($10M) as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $20M. This represents the quantitative anchor point given the nascent stage and available data.

The triangulated valuation range is $17M (low) to $23M (median $20M), centered on the stage-progression output. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier for this specific sector and stage transition, limited comp visibility with only two private reference points, and absence of public market benchmarks. The range reflects conservative positioning around the stage-progression result rather than validation across multiple independent methods. Investors should treat this range as preliminary pending deeper operational diligence and clearer articulation of comparable company multiples within consumer tech hardware.
